info.title: AI Authoring API
info.description:
The AI Test Authoring API allows you to manage AI-authored test cases, test suites, and test schedules. Use these methods to create, retrieve, update, delete, and run your AI-authored tests programmatically.

Refer to [Getting Started](/dev/api) for Authentication and Server information.

## What You'll Need

- A Sauce Labs enterprise account ([Log in](https://accounts.saucelabs.com/am/XUI/#login/))
- Your Sauce Labs [username and access key](https://app.saucelabs.com/user-settings)

get.description: Downloads a stored artifact (e.g. screenshot) by its UUID. Supports both dashed and dashless UUID formats.

schema.description: Artifact UUID (accepts both dashed and dashless formats)

parameters.description: Artifact UUID (accepts both dashed and dashless formats)
parameters.name: id

detail.description: Human-readable error description with actionable context
Human-readable error description with actionable context

get.description: Returns a paginated list of test cases. Supports filtering by search term, date range, user, team, and test suite.

schema.description: Case-insensitive search term matched against the test case name

parameters.description: Case-insensitive search term matched against the test case name
parameters.name: search

schema.description: ISO 8601 start date filter — only return test cases created on or after this date

parameters.description: ISO 8601 start date filter — only return test cases created on or after this date
parameters.name: startDate

schema.description: ISO 8601 end date filter — only return test cases created on or before this date

parameters.description: ISO 8601 end date filter — only return test cases created on or before this date
parameters.name: endDate

parameters.description: Filter by creator user UUID
parameters.name: userId

schema.description: Filter by team UUID

parameters.description: Filter by team UUID

schema.description: Filter by test suite UUID(s). Pass "null" to find unassigned test cases. Accepts a single value or an array.

parameters.description: Filter by test suite UUID(s). Pass "null" to find unassigned test cases. Accepts a single value or an array.
parameters.name: testSuiteId

schema.description: Filter by tag(s). Returns test cases that have at least one of the specified tags. Accepts a single value or an array.

parameters.description: Filter by tag(s). Returns test cases that have at least one of the specified tags. Accepts a single value or an array.
parameters.name: tags

schema.description: Number of results to skip (for offset-based pagination). Defaults to 0.

parameters.description: Number of results to skip (for offset-based pagination). Defaults to 0.
parameters.name: skip

schema.description: Maximum number of results to return. Set to 0 to only retrieve the total count.

parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return. Set to 0 to only retrieve the total count.
parameters.name: limit

post.description: Starts a test case run. Optionally target a specific revision by appending `/{revisionId}` to the path.

schema.description: Test case ObjectId (24-character hex string)

parameters.description: Test case ObjectId (24-character hex string)
parameters.name: id

buildName.description: Sauce Labs build name to group the run under (max 100 characters)

scTunnelName.description: Name of an active Sauce Connect tunnel to route traffic through

capabilities.description: W3C WebDriver capabilities object (e.g. browserName, platformName, browserVersion)

targets.description: List of browser/device targets to run the test against. Each entry specifies a capabilities object.

teamId.description: Team UUID that the run belongs to

userId.description: UUID of the user who initiated the run

testCaseId.description: ObjectId of the test case that was run

build.description: Sauce Labs build name grouping this run

id.description: Internal AI Authoring job ID for this run job

sauceJobId.description: Pre-generated Sauce Labs job ID (UUID) used to create a test case run. Use it to correlate with Sauce Labs Dashboard, job APIs or for live preview. Matches the actual Sauce Labs job ID.

capabilities.description: W3C WebDriver capabilities (e.g. browserName, platformName, browserVersion)

isRdc.description: Whether this target runs on a real device (true) or an emulator/VM (false)

url.description: Direct URL to the job in the Sauce Labs dashboard

isRdc.description: Whether the job ran on a real device

success.description: Whether all assertions in the job passed

error.description: Error message if the job failed or encountered an infrastructure issue

jobs.description: Individual Sauce Labs job results (one per target)

creationDate.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the run was created

testUrl.description: Base URL the test navigated to

post.description: Kicks off an asynchronous test case generation task. Use the returned `taskId` to poll for status via `GET /testcases/generate/{taskId}`.

testSuiteId.description: UUID (dashed or dashless). Dashes are stripped automatically.

tags.description: User-defined tags to assign to the generated test case. Up to 20 tags, each at most 60 characters.

capabilities.description: W3C WebDriver capabilities object (e.g. browserName, platformName, browserVersion)

testUrl.description: Fully qualified URL to test against (max 2048 characters)

scTunnelName.description: Name of an active Sauce Connect tunnel to route traffic through

intent.description: Natural language test prompt describing what the test should do (1–20 000 characters)

maxSteps.description: Maximum number of AI actions during generation (1–200).

timeout.description: Generation timeout in milliseconds (from 1 minute to 1 hour).

202.description: Generation task accepted. The body contains the `taskId` and the `sauceJobId` of the Sauce Labs session; the `Location` header points to the status endpoint for this task.

Location.description: Relative URL of the generation status endpoint, `/testcases/generate/{taskId}`.

sauceJobId.description: Pre-generated Sauce Labs job ID (UUID) of the WebDriver session driven during generation. Can be used to correlate with the Sauce Labs dashboard or job APIs. Matches the actual Sauce Labs job ID on both VDC (virtual devices and browsers) and RDC (real devices).

data.description: Result of accepting a generation request. `sauceJobId` (UUID) is the Sauce Labs job ID of the WebDriver session driven during generation, for correlating with Sauce Labs. Applies to both VDC and real device (RDC) sessions.

400.description:
- Invalid request body
- Test suite not found
- Sauce Connect tunnel not found

detail.description: Human-readable error description with actionable context
Human-readable error description with actionable context
Human-readable error description with actionable context
Human-readable error description with actionable context

get.description:
Returns the current status of an asynchronous generation task. While in progress, includes the test steps and AI reasoning captured so far (partial; updates as the task runs). On completion, includes the generated test case id (fetch the full test case for its final steps and reasoning). On failure, includes the underlying task error along with the steps and reasoning captured before it failed.

Polling: poll every 2-3 seconds while the task is `QUEUED` or `IN_PROGRESS`, and stop once the status is terminal (`COMPLETED` or `FAILED`).

schema.description: Task ID

parameters.description: Task ID
parameters.name: taskId

post.description: When `isSecret` is true, the value is persisted in a separate collection and is never returned by GET endpoints.

testCaseId.description: Required when scope=testCase. Must be omitted for other scopes.

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the variable (max 1000 characters).

isSecret.description: When true, the value is stored separately in variableSecrets and never returned in GET /variables responses.

value.description: Plaintext value (1+ characters). For secrets, the value is stored separately and never returned in GET /variables responses.

patch.description: Toggling `isSecret` in either direction accepts an optional new `value`. When omitted, the existing value is moved across the variable/secret boundary so a UI toggle flip works without re-supplying it. In both directions the secret row is created or deleted accordingly. Requires `expectedLastUpdate` (echo the value from the prior GET) to detect concurrent edits; returns 412 on mismatch. Modification is only permitted from the team that owns the variable.

schema.description: Variable UUID

parameters.description: Variable UUID
parameters.name: id

name.description: New variable name (1–255 characters; letters, digits, and underscores only)

description.description: New description (max 1000 characters). Send an empty string to clear it; omit to leave it unchanged.

isSecret.description: Toggle the secret flag. A new `value` is optional in either direction: when omitted, the existing value is moved across the variable/secret boundary so a UI toggle flip works without re-supplying it.

expectedLastUpdate.description: The `lastUpdate` value from the prior GET response (ISO 8601). Used for optimistic concurrency control — the request returns 412 if the variable has been modified since.

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the variable.

value.description: Plaintext value. Absent (not null) when isSecret is true.

lastModifierUserId.description: UUID of the user who last modified the variable

lastModifierUserName.description: Display name of the last modifier

creationDate.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the variable was created

lastUpdate.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent modification
